novel_features.name: Balanced recent slice
novel_features.description: Pulls N freshest 4-5 star reviews and N freshest 1-2 star reviews for a company in one call, so agents can quote a balanced view without two separate scrapes.
novel_features.rationale: Trustpilot has no single endpoint that returns a good+bad mix; one HTTP path biases the sample. Two filtered pulls merged locally are the only path.
novel_features.why_it_matters: When an agent is summarizing what consumers say about a company in the last 30 days, this is the one command that returns a balanced citable sample.

novel_features.name: One-shot agent bundle
novel_features.description: Returns a single JSON payload with company metadata, TrustScore, Trustpilot's own AI summary, top recent good and bad reviews, and the 5-bin rating histogram — everything an external agent needs in one call.
novel_features.rationale: Composes three Trustpilot endpoints plus one local aggregation; designed as the single MCP tool external research agents invoke.
novel_features.why_it_matters: If you only call one Trustpilot command from another agent, call this one. It is purpose-built for last30days-style story enrichment.

novel_features.name: TrustScore + sentiment drift
novel_features.description: Week-over-week TrustScore, 1-star %, 5-star %, and review volume reconstructed from locally synced reviews. Trustpilot exposes only the current TrustScore; this surfaces history.
novel_features.rationale: Local SQLite aggregation over synced reviews bucketed by ISO week. The API offers no historical-trustscore endpoint.
novel_features.why_it_matters: Use before claiming a company's reputation is improving or declining; one query gives you the weekly trend with star-mix breakdown.

novel_features.name: Multi-company comparison
novel_features.description: Side-by-side TrustScore, review velocity, and 1/5-star mix across N companies over a chosen window, all read from the local synced store.
novel_features.rationale: Cross-domain join over synced reviews; no existing scraper supports multi-company queries.
novel_features.why_it_matters: Best command for landscape analysis before recommending a vendor or writing a comparison piece.

novel_features.name: Review-velocity surge
novel_features.description: Detects statistically significant spikes in total review volume or 1-star volume against a rolling baseline using Z-scores over locally synced rows.
novel_features.rationale: Mechanical statistical test on synced rows; review pile-ons usually precede news cycles, and no scraper surfaces them.
novel_features.why_it_matters: Run this before publishing on a company; a fresh 1-star surge is the headline you would otherwise miss.

novel_features.name: Offline review FTS
novel_features.description: Full-text search over synced review titles, bodies, and business replies with star, language, and date filters. FTS5-backed, works offline.
novel_features.rationale: Trustpilot's UI has no per-company review-text search; FTS5 over the local store is the only way to grep N years of reviews.
novel_features.why_it_matters: When the question is 'what did 1-star reviewers say about refunds in the last 90 days', this is the only command that answers it.

novel_features.name: Business-reply gap
novel_features.description: Reply rate by star bucket over synced reviews, plus a listing of unreplied 1-stars when --unreplied is set.
novel_features.rationale: Every existing scraper carries the reply field; none aggregates reply rate by star bucket.
novel_features.why_it_matters: Companies that ignore 1-stars predict customer-support failure; this is the one-line audit for it.

novel_features.name: Country-of-reviewer mix
novel_features.description: Reviewer-country distribution over a window with per-country count, average rating, and 1-star rate.
novel_features.rationale: consumerCountry is in every review payload but never aggregated by existing tools.
novel_features.why_it_matters: Use when sentiment differs by region; the country breakdown is the first signal of a localized incident.

novel_features.name: Trustpilot topic-AI passthrough
novel_features.description: Surfaces Trustpilot's own pre-computed topic AI summaries (e.g., 'shipping', 'price', 'condition') as JSON.
novel_features.rationale: Pure passthrough of an existing pageProps field; the absorb manifest already covers the top-level AI summary but not these per-topic clusters.
novel_features.why_it_matters: When you need labeled clusters of what reviewers actually talk about, Trustpilot already did the clustering — this surfaces it.

novel_features.name: Similar-companies sweep
novel_features.description: Takes the 8 'similar businesses' Trustpilot returns for a company, fetches each one's info in parallel, and ranks them by TrustScore and total reviews.
novel_features.rationale: Builds on absorbed info --similar; the Trustpilot site requires 9 manual page loads to assemble this table.
novel_features.why_it_matters: Auto-discovered competitor set with metrics — perfect for prospecting or a competitor list you didn't curate.

novel_features.name: Sync delta
novel_features.description: Lists reviews that arrived since the last sync, bucketed by star rating, so an agent can poll for fresh customer feedback without re-fetching everything.
novel_features.rationale: Compares reviews.publishedAt against sync_cursors.lastSyncedAt; agent polling pattern with no equivalent on the Trustpilot site.
novel_features.why_it_matters: Best for scheduled agents that want the delta, not the full archive.

novel_features.name: Category leaderboard
novel_features.description: Ranks the companies in a Trustpilot category by TrustScore (with an optional minimum-review floor).
novel_features.rationale: The absorbed categories command returns the navigation list unranked; ranking by TrustScore + review-volume floor turns navigation into a query.
novel_features.why_it_matters: When you want 'best online bookstore on Trustpilot with at least 100 reviews', this is the one query.

narrative.headline: Every Trustpilot review surface, plus the local SQLite database and balanced good-and-bad agent bundle no other Trustpilot tool ships.
narrative.value_prop: Pulls public Trustpilot reviews into a local SQLite store with FTS5 over titles, bodies, and business replies. Designed to pair with research agents like last30days: one call returns a balanced good-and-bad sample for an entity along with TrustScore, Trustpilot's own AI summary, and the rating histogram.
narrative.auth_narrative: Trustpilot is fronted by AWS WAF, so plain HTTP is blocked. Run `trustpilot-pp-cli auth login --chrome` once to harvest the `aws-waf-token` cookie via a one-shot headless Chrome; the CLI persists it locally and refreshes automatically when it expires (every 5-15 minutes). No paid API subscription required.

quickstart.comment: One-time cookie harvest via headless Chrome; clears the AWS WAF challenge.
Resolve a company name to its canonical Trustpilot domain (e.g. www.thriftbooks.com).
TrustScore, total reviews, rating histogram, and Trustpilot's own AI summary.
Balanced recent slice — the headline command for agent integrations.
Pull up to 500 recent reviews into the local SQLite store for offline analysis.
FTS5 grep on synced reviews — no remote call once sync is current.

troubleshoots.symptom: Every request returns 403 with HTML body
troubleshoots.fix: Run `trustpilot-pp-cli auth login --chrome` to refresh the WAF cookie. Tokens expire after 5-15 minutes; the CLI auto-refreshes on 403 but only if a Chrome is available locally.
The `x-nextjs-data: 1` header was dropped. Re-run with `--debug` and confirm the request headers include it; if missing, rebuild the binary.

troubleshoots.symptom: Pagination stops after ~200 pages on a company with millions of reviews
troubleshoots.fix: Pass `--bust-cutoff` (default on for sync), which iterates `stars=1..5` × `languages` to multiply the effective depth.

troubleshoots.symptom: `drift` or `surge` returns 'no data — run sync first'
troubleshoots.fix: Run `trustpilot-pp-cli sync <domain> --max-pages 25` first; these commands read from the local SQLite store, not the API.

narrative.when_to_use: Reach for Trustpilot CLI when an agent or researcher needs consumer reviews for a company by domain or name. Best fit: enriching a research narrative with a balanced recent good+bad sample, tracking TrustScore drift over time, comparing several companies in one call, or grepping synced reviews for a term (`refund`, `chargeback`) with star and date filters. Pairs cleanly with last30days for story enrichment.

recipes.title: Balanced last-30-day sample for last30days
recipes.explanation: One call returns 5 fresh good and 5 fresh bad reviews — the canonical citable sample for a story.

recipes.title: One-call agent bundle (most efficient)
recipes.explanation: Composes info + top-recent + AI summary + histogram into a single payload; `--select` keeps the response under a few KB for deeply-nested fields.

recipes.title: 12-week trust score and 1-star drift
recipes.explanation: Reconstruct weekly TrustScore from synced reviews; spot inflection points the Trustpilot site never shows.

recipes.title: Compare three competitors for a Monday landscape sweep
recipes.explanation: Side-by-side TrustScore + review velocity + 1/5-star mix across the cohort, read from local store.

recipes.title: Grep synced 1-star reviews for refund complaints
recipes.explanation: FTS5 query — works offline once sync is current; combine with `--select rating,title,publishedAt,text` for narrow output.
